content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

October 12, 1990

Joan Mihay, Treasurer  
Ben Luna for Council Committee  
785 Cabrillo Place  
Morro Bay, CA 93442

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-90-590

Dear Ms. Mihay:

We have received your letter dated September 11, 1990 seeking advice regarding the responsibilities of the Voters Initiative Committee ("the committee") under the campaign contributions and use-of-campaign-funds provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act").1 You asked whether the committee was permitted to make campaign contributions to mayoral and city council candidates, to issue publications reflecting each candidate's position on a local ballot measure, and to make various expenditures of campaign funds showing the committee's candidate endorsements on slate mailers and yard signs.

We are unable to respond to your query. Under Regulation 18329, copy enclosed, the Fair Political Practices Commission is authorized to provide advice only to any person whose duties under the Act are in question. (Regulation 18329(b)(1), 18329(c)(1).) We cannot provide advice about another person's duties. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B), 18329(c)(4)(C).)

On the basis of the facts presented, it appears that the Voters Initiative Committee, or its authorized representative, is the appropriate party to seek advice on the question of how the committee's funds may be properly expended under the Act. On this question there does not appear to be any duty or responsibility that pertains to you as committee treasurer of a city council candidate about which we can provide advice.

Given the pendency of the November election, you were provided the above information by telephone, accompanied by the suggestion that you contact the Voters Initiative Committee and have

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

them request advice from this agency. This letter therefore serves to confirm such telephone advice.

I am sorry that we are unable to provide you with the assistance that you requested. If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:plh
Enclosure
